Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Agent: reset error counter on success, log recovery and periodic errors

The agent suppressed all errors after 5 failures, so if the panel
wasn't ready during MC server boot, the agent silently gave up forever.
Now resets error counter on first successful POST (with recovery log),
and logs every 60th error instead of going completely silent.

Add bulk actions on opportunities: Close Lost, Forecast, Stage

- Checkbox on each opp card + Select All toggle
- Bulk action bar appears when opps selected
- Bulk Close Lost: one-click close multiple opps as Closed Lost
- Bulk Update Forecast: set Group Forecast Category on multiple opps
- Bulk Change Stage: move multiple opps to any stage
- Uses SFDC Composite API for batch updates (up to 25 at once)
- Shows selected count, opp names in confirmation panel

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Refactor: Audit-runner architecture - clean UI/CLI separation

Extract business logic from API server to standalone CLI scripts:
- Add audit:rank script (rankings report with text/json formats)
- Add audit:history script (score trends with text/json/chart formats)
- Add commit script (atomic commit with normalization + validation)

Refactor API endpoints to spawn npm scripts instead of inline logic:
- /api/history → spawns audit:history script
- /api/trip-audit → spawns trip-audit script (was bypassing CLI)
- /api/commit-file → spawns commit script

Standardize CLI flags across audit scripts:
- Add --force flag to opus-audit, gpt-audit (skip change detection)
- Consistent --provider, --format, --audit-dir flag patterns

Update verification skills:
- /techdebt: detect API endpoints with business logic (>20 lines)
- /doc-sync: verify UI button → npm script mappings

All UI actions now have CLI equivalents. API server is thin wrapper
that validates input, spawns scripts, and streams progress via WebSocket.

commit.message:
fix: race week down to 1.5h, proper taper progression

Race week (W08) fixed:
- Was: Openers + 5× Endurance 70min = 6.5h (athlete would be tired)
- Now: Openers 41min + 1 easy Endurance 49min = 1.5h (arrive fresh)
- Filler days in race week default to Rest Day
- Only 1 easy ride (2nd filler day = Wed) for leg activation
- Matches block-builder spec: Mon OFF, Tue Openers, Wed Easy, Thu-Fri OFF

Volume taper progression for Julie Dixon (8-week plan):
  W01-02: 9.6-10.0h (Load — Race Sim + Blended VO2)
  W03:    6.1h       (Recovery)
  W04-05: 9.5-9.7h  (Load — Race Sim + Threshold Accumulation)
  W06:    6.2h       (Recovery)
  W07:    2.8h       (Taper — Openers + easy Endurance)
  W08:    1.5h       (Race week — Openers + 1 easy ride)

641 tests passing, 0 failures. 53 ZWO files delivered.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: show completed workout state on Today page instead of rest day card

Query scheduled_workouts for completed status before checking for scheduled,
so a completed session takes priority. Previously, completing a session updated
the scheduled_workout status to "completed" but getTodayWorkout only queried
for "scheduled" rows, causing it to return null and show the rest day card.

fix: redirect test output to file, only read on failure

When Claude runs the test suite during a mission, the full verbose
output was loaded into context even on success — wasting tokens with
no value. Instruct Claude to redirect output to /tmp/test-output.txt
and only cat it when the exit code is non-zero.

Fixes https://github.com/Anantys-oss/koan/issues/1133

commit.message:
문서: 바코드매칭실패 BARCODEGOODS 위치설정불일치 오류 문서 추가

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

style(cli): worktree indicator as inverse badge (#338)

* style(cli): make worktree indicator a bold inverse badge instead of plain prefix

Use Ink's inverse+bold on magenta background to render the worktree name
as a visually distinct pill/badge that's impossible to miss. Non-worktree
repos still show the normal dim git icon + branch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cli): resolve git paths before comparing for worktree detection

git rev-parse --git-dir and --git-common-dir can return one relative
and one absolute path when CWD is a subdirectory of the repo. The naive
string comparison treated them as different, causing isWorktree() to
return true in every non-root directory — showing the worktree badge
everywhere.

Fix: resolve() both paths before comparing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: simplified jeu flow — scan once, spin once, result banner then back to scanner

- State machine: loading → scanner → wheel → result → scanner (3s)
- Result shown as fixed orange banner at top, auto-dismisses after 3s
- game_plays table check before unlocking wheel (silent if already played)

  then updates profiles.points_balance + points_history

feat(cli): context budget widget, statusbar redesign, and git diff stats (#336)

* feat(cli): context budget widget, statusbar redesign, and git diff stats

Redesign the CLI status bar with vim-inspired mode placement, a visual
context window gauge, proper worktree support, and git diff stats.

StatusBar:
- Mode leftmost (vim-style), bold/colored/uppercase
- Context gauge with fill bar and green/yellow/red color coding
- Git shows +insertions -deletions instead of dirty file count
- Worktree shows named indicator (wt:name) instead of generic [wt]
- Nerd Font auto-detection (env var > terminal detection > config flag)
- No empty sections when context data unavailable

Git layer:
- getDiffStats() parses git diff HEAD --stat for line counts
- getWorktreeName() extracts name from git-dir worktree path

Context budget:
- getContextBreakdown() categorizes tokens by role with API-accurate fill %
- /context command shows full per-role breakdown with progress bars
- useAgent tracks API-reported prompt tokens for real context window size

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cli): address Greptile review — clamp bar fill, module-level nerd font detection, HEAD fallback

- P0: Clamp filled/pct in /context bar() to prevent RangeError from
  negative repeat count when estimated tokens exceed API-reported total
- P2: Move nerd font env detection to module level (evaluated once at
  load, not on every render)
- P2: getDiffStats falls back to git diff --stat when HEAD doesn't exist
  on brand-new repos with no commits

Signal Forecast is installable as a standalone PWA. Agents can add
the app to their home screen and launch directly into /signal-forecast
with MSA branding and no browser chrome.

* feat: service worker setup — Serwist integration for PWA caching (#291)

Add service worker with Serwist (next-pwa successor) for precaching
app shell and Signal Forecast route with runtime caching strategies.
NetworkFirst for navigation, StaleWhileRevalidate for API, CacheFirst
for static assets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: offline pull cache — IndexedDB storage for Signal Forecast (#292)

Cache last 10 saved pulls in IndexedDB for offline access. Pull history
and detail views fall back to cached data when offline, with staleness
indicators. Uses idb wrapper, useOnlineStatus hook, and LRU eviction.

Hook intercepts beforeinstallprompt, tracks pull count in localStorage.

commit.message:
feat: enforce filemtime() version in wp_enqueue_style/script gate (#236)

Add PreToolUse hook that blocks static version strings like '1.0.0'
in wp_enqueue_style() and wp_enqueue_script() calls inside PHP files.
Forces filemtime() or a dynamic alternative to ensure cache busting
whenever theme assets are modified.

Allowed: filemtime(), $variables, CONSTANTS, null/false, CDN URLs
Blocked: any quoted static string of digits/dots as the 4th arg

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: change all canonical URLs to www.tecknow.news

Vercel redirects tecknow.news → www.tecknow.news but all canonical
URLs, sitemaps, JSON-LD, and OG tags used the non-www version.
This caused Google Search Console to report "Pagina alternativa
con etiqueta canonica adecuada" and refuse to index 38 pages.

Changed https://tecknow.news → https://www.tecknow.news in:
- constants.ts, layout.tsx, robots.ts, sitemap.ts
- news-sitemap.xml, feed.xml, [slug]/page.tsx
- categoria/[slug]/page.tsx, ArticleJsonLd.tsx
- email.ts, rss-parser.ts

Clean URL: /api/mkt/* → /mkt/* (api.flowxp.ai/mkt/ingest)

Remove redundant /api prefix — subdomain is already api.flowxp.ai.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add procedural textures to all greenhouse surfaces for ZED depth

The ZED 2i stereo camera needs surface texture to produce good depth
maps. Without it, flat solid-color surfaces produce empty/noisy depth.

Added 6 procedural texture maps:
  ground_soil.png (1024x1024) — brown soil with gravel variation
  wall_polycarbonate.png (512x512) — white panels with seam lines
  crop_leaves.png (512x512) — dense green leaf pattern
  crop_leaves_alt.png (512x512) — alternate shade for row variation
  crate_wood.png (256x256) — wood plank grain
  rail_steel.png (256x128) — brushed galvanized steel

Material fixes for better stereo matching:
  Walls: roughness 0.3 → 0.85 (reduce specular reflection)
  Rails: metalness 0.7 → 0.3 (reduce mirror effect)
  Roof beams: metalness 0.6 → 0.2 (same)

generate_textures.py creates all textures procedurally via numpy+PIL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: Peaceful white noise — brown noise base, gentle natural sounds

- Replaced harsh white noise with smooth brown noise (integrated random)
- Stereo buffers for more natural spatial feel
- Rain: soft hiss with gentle high-frequency shimmer, like rain on a window
- Ocean: deep warm bass with dual slow wave modulation
- Forest: very quiet wind rustling with gentle frequency modulation
- Fire: warm low-mid crackling with subtle modulation
- Lower base volume (0.3x) for peaceful ambient feel
